title: Iran’s Strategy: Expand the War, Increase the Cost, Outlast Trump
summary: The Islamic Republic is aiming to draw out the conflict and broaden the fighting. That would force President Trump to risk more casualties and more political capital.

title: Israel Seizes Chance to Crush Hezbollah and Remake Mideast Amid Iran War
summary: With the war against Iran underway, and the U.S. military as a powerful ally, the Israeli government is seizing its chance to move against other adversaries.

title: Labor Secretary’s Top Aides Forced Out
summary: The two aides to Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer had been on leave during an investigation of misconduct at the department.

title: Trump Administration, in Reversal, Tries to Continue Fight Against Law Firms
summary: The administration told a court on Monday that it was abandoning its defense of executive orders targeting the firms. But on Tuesday, the Justice Department abruptly changed its position.

title: Congestion Pricing Wins in Court After Lengthy Battle With Trump
summary: The victory removes a major threat to the New York City tolling program, which still faces other lawsuits.

title: DHS Investigates Gregory Bovino’s Remarks About Prosecutor’s Jewish Faith
summary: Mr. Bovino, who was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdowns in American cities, was reported to have made disparaging comments in reference to the U.S. attorney in Minnesota, who is an Orthodox Jew.

title: Minnesota Sues The Trump Administration Over Medicaid Amid Fraud Scandal
summary: The Trump administration cited widespread fraud in state social service programs. Minnesota officials said they were victims of “political punishment.”

title: Apalachee High School Shooting: Colin Gray Found Guilty of Murder
summary: A jury found that Colin Gray, the father of the suspect, bore criminal responsibility for the attack at a Georgia school by failing to heed warnings of his son’s struggles.

title: Billionaires Spending More Than Ever on Miami Real Estate
summary: The ultrawealthy are vying for a limited number of exclusive properties on the islands and shorelines of South Florida.

title: Texas and North Carolina Elections Will Test Older House Democrats’ Staying Power
summary: Starting in North Carolina on Tuesday, older House Democrats face a test of their staying power and the appetite of voters to replace them.

title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Texas, Arkansas and North Carolina
summary: Midterm season is kicking off with hard-fought Senate and House primary contests that include battles over political style and ideology, age and scandal.

title: Crockett vs. Talarico. Progressive vs. Moderate. Right?
summary: The Texas Democratic Senate primary is more about persona than policy, and the same is true for many other races.

title: Seven Takeaways From the Clintons’ Epstein Depositions
summary: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton was defiant. Former President Bill Clinton spoke of President Trump’s ties to Epstein. A Republican raised a conspiracy theory.

title: An Epstein Associate Steps Down From Japanese Government Tech Project
summary: Joichi Ito’s involvement in a publicly funded Japanese initiative had come under scrutiny after new details revealed his close relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

title: Ford and G.M. Face a Dilemma as China Excels in Electric Vehicles
summary: General Motors, Ford and other established automakers risk becoming relics if they don’t catch up to Chinese carmakers and technology companies in electric vehicles and self-driving cars.

title: Tariffs Confound Small Businesses Again
summary: Chocolate makers and fig-paste importers are facing a tangle of unknowns, including whether to seek refunds for tariffs invalidated by the Supreme Court.

title: The Psychoanalyst Stephen Grosz Brings His Readers Into the Room
summary: Stephen Grosz’s books show a new generation the inner workings of psychoanalysis.

title: In South Carolina Measles Outbreak, Vaccine Skepticism Led to Largest Outbreak Since 2000
summary: Spartanburg County in South Carolina is ground zero for the largest measles outbreak since 2000. One school has a vaccination rate of 21 percent.

title: In a World Order Defined by Trump, the Key to Europe’s Defense Is Germany
summary: In the wake of the U.S. bombing of Iran and its dismissal of European allies, an anxious continent’s best chance at security runs through its largest economy.

title: Germany Has Ratcheted Up Defense Spending. In Europe, That Worries Some People.
summary: Soon the country’s armed forces budget could exceed those of Britain and France combined. In Paris, there are concerns that European “strategic autonomy” will have a German accent.

title: Colleges See Spike in Students With Disabilities, Including Elite Schools
summary: Universities from Harvard to Hampshire have admitted significantly more students with disabilities over the last decade, as diagnoses for A.D.H.D. and anxiety increase.

title: First 2-K Seats in Mamdani’s Signature Program Will Be in Diverse Areas
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has pledged to create a free, universal child care system during his tenure. On Tuesday, he announced where first wave of seats would become available for 2-year-olds.

title: Mamdani’s Parks Dept. Aims to Offer More Recreation With Smaller Budget
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s Parks Department plans to become New York City’s “agency of affordability,” though the mayor has not increased the agency’s funding.

title: Nine Law Firms Surrendered. Four Law Firms Won.
summary: The four law firms that fought Trump’s executive orders have forced him to retreat.

title: Trump’s Head-on-a-Pike Foreign Policy
summary: Ben Rhodes, a contributing Times Opinion writer, discusses why Trump waged war on Iran — and why his predecessors didn’t dare to.

title: The Midterms Begin During War
summary: We’re following the first primaries of the midterm elections.

title: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
summary: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.

title: Offering Warmth and Care to the Homeless, as Cold Deaths Mount
summary: New outreach teams from New York City’s public hospital system include a clinical professional to evaluate the needs of homeless New Yorkers.

title: Justin Timberlake Drunk-Driving Footage Won’t Be Released Just Yet
summary: The authorities in Sag Harbor, N.Y., said they would not yet release footage of the singer’s 2024 arrest while they work out a resolution with his lawyers.

title: Pizza Hut Take Fans on a Trip to the Past
summary: With heaps of nostalgia but little promotion by their parent chain, Pizza Hut Classics take their fervent fans on a time trip back to a simpler, warmer era.
